title: Broad Phase, Circle, Rect, Mask, Seam, and Swept Collision Detection · Runtime Lens: Collision Shapes
summary: The game tests every pair, uses transformed Rect overlap as exact geometry, constructs masks every frame, misses seam pairs, and lets fast bullets tunnel through small rocks. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the collision shapes increment in layered collision detection pipeline. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed…
objectives: Choose circle, AABB, rotated bound, polygon, or mask from acceptable false positives, cost, and player expectation.
Bound count, speed, size, spawn interval, aim burden, cue clarity, and selectable assistance as separate difficulty inputs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example Asteroids team handles case 9881 during a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling. Build deterministic Python evidence to layer broad-phase pruning, circle or rectangle checks, masks where justified, seam cases, and swept tests for tunneling; record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then replace an audio-only collision cue with synchronized text, shape, and optional sound evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. A defensible solution applies Collision Shapes, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to layered collision detection pipeline.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-collision-detection-2
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-collision-detection-2.
# Case 7417: a headless continuous-integration replay.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state.
def worked_asteroids_collision_detection_2(distance_squared: float = 400.0, radius_sum: float = 24.0, broad_phase_pairs: int = 18, narrow_phase_pairs: int = 3, swept_tested: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "playerworked2",
        "seed7417",
        "input2dekrow",
        "frameworked27417",
        "state7417worked2",
        "playtest2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if distance_squared < 0.0 or radius_sum < 0.0:
        return False, distance_squared, "collision-domain"
    if narrow_phase_pairs > broad_phase_pairs:
        return False, narrow_phase_pairs, "phase-inversion"
    overlaps = distance_squared <= radius_sum * radius_sum
    if not swept_tested:
        return False, narrow_phase_pairs, "tunneling-gap"
    if broad_phase_pairs < 1:
        return False, broad_phase_pairs, "missing-broad-phase"
    assert narrow_phase_pairs <= broad_phase_pairs
    return True, int(overlaps), "layered-collision"

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-collision-shapes-sr8nh4
# Competency: asteroids-collision-shapes.
# Case 3998: a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial.
# Operating constraint: preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets.
# Changed case: run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save.
def evidence_asteroids_collision_shapes_sr8nh4(distance_squared: float = 400.0, radius_sum: float = 24.0, broad_phase_pairs: int = 18, narrow_phase_pairs: int = 3, swept_tested: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_sr8nh4 = "sr8nh4-4hn8rs-3998"
    assert evidence_variant_sr8nh4
    evidence_axes_sr8nh4 = (
        "playersr8nh4",
        "seed3998",
        "input4hn8rs",
        "framesr8nh43998",
        "state3998sr8nh4",
        "playtest4hn8rs3998",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_sr8nh4)) == 6
    if distance_squared < 0.0 or radius_sum < 0.0:
        return False, distance_squared, "collision-domain"
    if narrow_phase_pairs > broad_phase_pairs:
        return False, narrow_phase_pairs, "phase-inversion"
    overlaps = distance_squared <= radius_sum * radius_sum
    if not swept_tested:
        return False, narrow_phase_pairs, "tunneling-gap"
    if broad_phase_pairs < 1:
        return False, broad_phase_pairs, "missing-broad-phase"
    assert narrow_phase_pairs <= broad_phase_pairs
    return True, int(overlaps), "layered-collision"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Collision Shapes model · Theory Collision Shapes
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Collision Shapes: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory Asteroids team handles case 1595 during a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling. Build deterministic Python evidence to layer broad-phase pruning, circle or rectangle checks, masks where justified, seam cases, and swept tests for tunneling; preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets; then inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Choose circle, AABB, rotated bound, polygon, or mask from acceptable false positives, cost, and player expectation.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Choose circle, AABB, rotated bound, polygon, or mask from acceptable false positives, cost, and player expectation.
